titleStmt.title: The Ambassador in China ( Stuart ) to the Secretary of State

div.head: The Ambassador in China ( Stuart ) to the Secretary of State

opener.dateline:
Nanking , February 9, 1948—7 p. m.
[Received February 9—9:02 a. m.]

div.p:
259. Altho we are cognizant of advantages from an intelligence standpoint of having consular officer travel in Communist areas North
China (reDeptel 201, February 6, 8 p. m.), we consider that such action would have adverse effect upon position Central Govt and would thus be unwise at this time. As we have reported to Dept there has recently been an increase of rumors to the effect that peace talks may be reopened between Kuomintang and Communists either directly or through Soviet mediation. In face of these widespread rumors we do not feel that any official American agent could visit Communist areas at this time without visit becoming known and immediately linked with foregoing rumors. We therefore recommend that no action be taken at this time on suggestions included in reference despatch from Peiping.
